rows: High-Tensile Galvanised C-Purlins (Sections) are roll formed from high-tensile Zinc coated steel with base steel Thickness
They are suitable for roofing and wall cladding supports and for structural frames of buildings. Due to the light weight and
high strength of the steel and the Zinc-coated surface. High-Tensile Galvanised C-Purlins are versatile and economical and
require minimal maintenance throughout the life span of the building.
Base Steel Thickness : 1.60 mm, 2.00 mm and 2.50 mm

DESIGN PRINCIPLE FOR PURLIN SELECTION TABLES
The purlin selection tables are derived basing on BS 5950 : Part 5 : 1987 “Code of
Practice for design of cold formed sections”.
1) Purlin is simply supported with pinned joints over supports.
The purlin selection tables are only applicable for roof pitch less than or equal
Besides dead load arising from roof self weight, the purlins are designed based
on a live load of 0.25KN/m2(Malaysian Building Bylaw Clause 63) and wind
i) 1.4 D.L. + 1.6 L.L. (The most severe inward loading combination)
ii) 1.0 D.L. + 1.4 W.L. (The most severe outward loading combination)
Under serviceability limit state the limiting deflection under total load is L/150
whereas under live load only is L/180 (where L is the purlin span).
The design strength Py for purlins is to be taken as the minimum yield strength

PURLIN SELECTION TABLES FOR ROOFING APPLICATION
The following purlin selection tables provide an easy reference for selection of appropriate
purlin size in accordance with the bay width or column spacing of the building.
These tables can be generally used in Singapore and Malaysia. However, for optimum
designs where specific roofi ng and insulation loads are to be used or for any other structure
or for application outside Singapore and Malaysia, contact CHYE HIN HARDWARE PTE
